히브리서 5:1.content: For every high priest, being taken from among men, is appointed for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.

히브리서 5:2.content: The high priest can deal gently with those who are ignorant and going astray, because he himself is also surrounded with weakness.

히브리서 5:3.content: Because of this, he must offer sacrifices for sins for the people, as well as for himself.

히브리서 5:4.content: Nobody takes this honor on himself, but he is called by God, just like Aaron was.

히브리서 5:5.content: So also Christ didn’t glorify himself to be made a high priest, but it was he who said to him, “You are my Son. Today I have become your father.”

히브리서 5:6.content: As he says also in another place, “You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.”

히브리서 5:7.content: He, in the days of his flesh, having offered up prayers and petitions with strong crying and tears to him who was able to save him from death, and having been heard for his godly fear,

히브리서 5:8.content: though he was a Son, yet learned obedience by the things which he suffered.

히브리서 5:9.content: Having been made perfect, he became to all of those who obey him the author of eternal salvation,

히브리서 5:10.content: named by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.

히브리서 5:11.content: About him we have many words to say, and hard to interpret, seeing you have become dull of hearing.

히브리서 5:12.content: For although by this time you should be teachers, you again need to have someone teach you the rudiments of the first principles of the revelations of God. You have come to need milk, and not solid food.

히브리서 5:13.content: For everyone who lives on milk is not experienced in the word of righteousness, for he is a baby.

히브리서 5:14.content: But solid food is for those who are full grown, who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern good and evil.
